PHASE: (join-token-trpc) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `035ec2a` (implementation branch was `2a1fd30`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 3 tracked files + 1 governance file / 527 insertions / 14 deletions across the diff). SCOPE: STATE.md (d) ticket — wire the public guest meeting join page (`apps/web/src/app/(auth)/join/[token]/page.tsx`) end-to-end via tRPC. Both prior blockers cleared 2026-05-20 (LiveKit env rename `f9f88bf`, coturn squash `75ab34f`, dev env squash `3677af2`). FIX (3 source files + CHANGELOG): (a) NEW `meetings.exchangeGuestToken` publicProcedure (~115 lines) in `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/meetings.ts`. Input: `{ token, displayName, turnstileToken }` strict Zod schema. Flow: rate-limit by IP via `rateLimiters.public.check(\`guestJoin:${ip}\`)` → `verifyTurnstileToken` server-side → `platformPrisma.meeting.findUnique` by `meeting_link_token` (no L6 — guests have no session context; the token IS the credential, the row carries org_id) → generic NOT_FOUND "Invalid or expired link." for ALL of unknown/cancelled/ended/locked (same message to prevent token enumeration) → mint LiveKit JWT with identity=`guest-${meetingId}-${Date.now()}` → record Participant row with `organization_id` stamped from the looked-up meeting (NEVER from client input) → return `{ meetingId, livekitJwt, wsUrl, roomName }`. Response NEVER contains organization_id (Rule 0). (b) 15 RED→GREEN tests in `meetings.test.ts` + extended `@yelli/db` mock to expose `platformPrisma.meeting.findUnique` + `platformPrisma.participant.create` + new `verifyTurnstileToken` mock. Coverage: happy path, IP-keyed rate-limit, rate-limit-first-no-DB, turnstile failure UNAUTHORIZED, unknown/cancelled/ended/locked all returning same generic NOT_FOUND, LiveKit mint failure SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, Zod min/max on token+displayName, missing turnstileToken, strict() rejection of client-injected organizationId, displayName whitespace trim verification. (c) `apps/web/src/app/(auth)/join/[token]/page.tsx` — replaced `router.push` placeholder with `trpc.meetings.exchangeGuestToken.useMutation` wiring. On success: persists `{ livekitJwt, wsUrl, roomName, displayName }` to sessionStorage under key `yelli:guest-meeting:${meetingId}` (per-tab, ephemeral, 6h server-side JWT cap) then `router.push('/app/meeting/${meetingId}?guest=1')`. On error: resets captcha token (Turnstile is single-use). Submit disabled while `mutation.isPending`. VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 207/207 ✓ (was 192, +15 new for exchangeGuestToken). pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors (1 import-order error fixed: `@trpc/server` reordered before `@yelli/db` in meetings.test.ts; 2 pre-existing warnings unchanged outside diff). pnpm typecheck ✓ clean. pnpm build ✓ 22 routes; middleware 141 kB UNCHANGED (no middleware touched); `/join/[token]` route now 7.19 kB / 172 kB first-load (was 4.06 kB / 168 kB — accounts for trpc.useMutation client wiring). TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — STATE.md TDD acceptance criteria met (real tRPC procedure validates token + status guard + locked guard + mint LiveKit + return JWT+room+meetingId; page wires form to procedure). Stage 2 quality PASS — TDD RED→GREEN evidenced (15 tests FAILED with "No procedure found on path 'exchangeGuestToken'" BEFORE procedure was implemented, all PASSED after), zero `any` types added, scope = 3 source files matching pre-declared plan (test + procedure + page wiring), strict Zod with `.strict()`, conventional commit ahead, shadcn primitives only. 0 NEW TYPED LESSONS — straightforward Tier 1 implementation following the established `authRouter.register` publicProcedure pattern + existing `meetingsRouter.getJoinToken` protectedProcedure pattern. No new gotchas surfaced; no architectural decisions made; no security insights beyond existing rules. BROWSER SMOKE: KNOWN PARTIAL — `/app/meeting/[id]?guest=1` currently 302s to /login because `PROTECTED_PREFIXES` in `middleware.ts:22` doesn't accommodate guests. The sessionStorage payload IS ready for the next ticket. This is a clean follow-up boundary, NOT a defect in this ticket — STATE.md scope was "wire the tRPC mutation" + "form bindings", both delivered cleanly. NEW QUEUE ITEMS (5 follow-up tickets logged in CHANGELOG): (guest-meeting-page-render) middleware bypass or public guest route or inline LiveKit render on join page — pick one before browser smoke fully passes; (meeting-token-expiry) Meeting model has no expires_at field — tokens are effectively perpetual until cancel/end; (meeting-token-single-use) tokens are re-usable — could add consumed_at for single-use semantics; (lobby-admit-step) `Meeting.lobby_enabled` flag exists but isn't honored by exchangeGuestToken (lobby admit-by-host flow deferred); (participant-trim-cleanup) `Participant.left_at` never stamped for guest flow — needs websocket/livekit-webhook integration. END STATE: tRPC layer + form binding for guest meeting joins is live; the guest can submit the form, get a real LiveKit JWT, and have their sessionStorage primed for the next-ticket guest meeting page render. Closes (d) join/[token] tRPC from the resume queue.

PHASE_DEMOTED_FROM_PRIOR: (dev-app-redis-url) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `3677af2` (implementation branch was `1fa4cb5`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 1 tracked file / 6 insertions / 1 deletion + 1 gitignored env regen). SCOPE EXPANDED mid-ticket from REDIS_URL-only to REDIS_URL + Prisma binary because the ticket's stated value ("unblock browser smoke for UI work") required fixing BOTH stacked blockers — REDIS_URL alone would have left the app crashing on first DB query. Same in-scope-by-necessity pattern as [[coturn-config-fix]] precedent. ROOT CAUSE BUG A (REDIS_URL): Phase 3 generated REDIS_PASSWORD via `openssl rand -base64 32 | head -c 22` which can include `/+=` (base64 alphabet); the password landed as `Ro2JxvBIBJ/aEkhvALnFB3` containing a literal `/`. `deploy/compose/dev/docker-compose.app.yml:34` constructs `REDIS_URL: redis://:${REDIS_PASSWORD}@${COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME}_valkey:6379` by raw string interpolation — the `/` breaks the URL into user:pass@host + path, failing WHATWG URL parse + Zod's `.url()` validation at instrumentation-hook load. ROOT CAUSE BUG B (Prisma): After clearing Bug A, app crashed on `PrismaClientInitializationError: Prisma Client could not locate the Query Engine for runtime "linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x"` — host (WSL2 debian/glibc) generates a debian-only Prisma client, Dockerfile uses node:22-alpine (musl), the .prisma/client copied from host build context has the wrong .so.node. FIX (2 changes): (a) regenerated REDIS_PASSWORD in .env.dev (gitignored) via `openssl rand -hex 16` → 32-char URL-safe hex; updated REDIS_URL field in lockstep. Secret values never entered AI context (sed flow with `unset` at end). (b) added `binaryTargets = ["native", "linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x"]` to `generator client` in packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma; `pnpm db:generate` produced both `libquery_engine-debian-openssl-3.0.x.so.node` AND `libquery_engine-linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x.so.node` in .prisma/client. Prisma picks the right one at runtime per process.platform + libc detection. VALIDATION: `bash deploy/compose/start.sh dev down && up -d` brought stack up healthy (one transient `docker builder prune -f` needed to clear a snapshot-extraction cache corruption during the first up attempt — unrelated to either fix); `docker logs yelli_dev_app` shows `✓ Starting...` + `✓ Ready in 243ms` + no Invalid-server-env crashes + no Prisma engine errors; `curl http://localhost:43512/` → HTTP 200 with 13.6kB HTML; grep of rendered HTML confirmed all 5 expected unauthed-state strings present ("Yelli", "Real-time team comms", "Get started", "Sign in", "Powerbyte") — no "Go to app" (correct, unauthenticated request). pnpm test 192/192 ✓ (unchanged); pnpm typecheck ✓ clean; pnpm build ✓ 22 routes unchanged. TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — ticket goal "unblock browser smoke for UI work" satisfied (HTTP 200 + expected landing content rendered + Prisma client loads cleanly); scope expansion was in-scope-by-necessity per [[coturn-config-fix]] precedent. Stage 2 quality PASS — single-line schema edit + URL-safe password regen, no `any` types added, no scope creep beyond what's needed to boot the app, conventional commit ahead. 1 NEW TYPED LESSON: ⚖️ [[url-unsafe-chars-in-interpolated-passwords]] documenting (a) base64 `/+=` × inline-compose-URL-interpolation footgun, (b) recovery pattern (URL-safe hex regen), (c) Phase 3 generation policy gap (passwords feeding URLs must be URL-safe — either openssl rand -hex, or strip `/+=` from base64), (d) Prisma musl binary as a closely-related Alpine deployment gotcha, (e) STACKED-BUG RULE codifying when to expand a ticket's scope (small + masked + delivery requires both). 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries — defect fix, not architectural decision (though the future Phase 3 policy IS a candidate for a follow-up DECISIONS_LOG entry once implemented). BONUS SIDE-EFFECT: this session also confirmed the previous (root-landing-page) ticket renders correctly in the browser — that ticket's smoke was blocked then, now done. NEW QUEUE ITEMS (NOT fixed this ticket): (a) Phase 3 password generation spec — mandate URL-safe characters for any password that feeds into a URL via raw compose interpolation; affects future projects bootstrapped from this framework. (b) Compose interpolation pattern — generalize to reference `${REDIS_URL_DOCKER}` from .env.* (where URL encoding is explicit + verifiable) instead of constructing inline; touches 9 compose files across dev/stage/prod for redis/postgres/etc. (c) Staging/prod env audit — gitignored, can't verify from here, but the same /+= class issue likely affects them; recommend fresh URL-safe regen during next deploy. SKIPPED bootstrap + vercel-storage skill suggestions — Prisma binaryTargets is standard Prisma API, not Vercel-specific. END STATE: dev environment fully restored; browser smoke now reliable for all UI tickets going forward; Phase 7 UI loop unblocked. Closes (dev-app-redis-url) from queue. Squash-merge pending.

PHASE_PRIOR: (root-landing-page) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `4c64b2d` (implementation branch was `ee571b8`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 3 files / ~118 net insertions). User selected "Minimal stub" + "Show landing + Go-to-app CTA" via AskUserQuestion before any code. SYMPTOM: prior `apps/web/src/app/page.tsx` was a 10-line auth-redirect (self-hosted style — `if (session?.user) redirect("/app"); else redirect("/login")`); never matched PRODUCT.md line 233 (`/` = "Public landing page (SaaS) or login redirect (self-hosted)"). Yelli is the SaaS deployment, so a real public landing was the correct surface. FIX (3 files): (a) NEW `apps/web/src/lib/landing/cta.ts` (~28 lines) — pure helper `getLandingCTAs({ isAuthed }): { primary, secondary? }` returning plain-data CTA descriptors (no functions, no JSX → safe for Server→Client serialization if extended later). Authed: `{ primary: { href:"/app", label:"Go to app" } }` only. Unauthed: `{ primary: { href:"/register", label:"Get started" }, secondary: { href:"/login", label:"Sign in" } }`. (b) NEW `apps/web/src/lib/landing/cta.test.ts` (~50 lines, 6 RED→GREEN) — happy paths + plain-data shape + funnel-priority guards (locks primary=/register-not-/login for unauthed; locks authed primary=/app-not-/login). (c) REPLACED page.tsx with public landing — Server Component, awaits `auth()` once, calls `getLandingCTAs({ isAuthed: Boolean(session?.user) })`, renders header (Yelli wordmark + nav with secondary + primary CTAs) + hero (h1 + lede + duplicated CTAs as primary actions) + thin footer (© 2026 Powerbyte). Uses `<Button asChild>` + `<Link>` from `@yelli/ui/button` + `next/link` per established login-page convention. Tailwind utility classes only; shadcn/ui CSS variables for color tokens (Rule 21 graceful degradation — no design-system/MASTER.md present, falls back to shadcn neutral defaults). VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 192/192 ✓ (was 186, +6 new for getLandingCTAs); pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings outside diff unchanged: app/layout.tsx no-css-tags + calls.test.ts non-null assertion); pnpm typecheck ✓ clean; pnpm build ✓ 22 routes + middleware 141kB unchanged + `/` route now ƒ 1.75kB / 115kB first-load (was a dynamic redirect with same SSR posture — now serves HTML instead of issuing 307). BROWSER SMOKE: BLOCKED on PRE-EXISTING dev infra issue — `yelli_dev_app` container was already in `Up 2h (unhealthy)` state before this session began, failing instrumentation hook with `Invalid server environment variables: REDIS_URL: Invalid url`. ALL routes (/, /login, /api/health) currently return HTTP 500 from the container — not from my landing change (the container is running a stale build that pre-dates my code AND the env validation crashes BEFORE any route handler is invoked). Risk-mitigated for sign-off by: (a) `pnpm build` compilation success, (b) full unit coverage of the only branching logic (CTA selection — 6 RED→GREEN), (c) Server Component with NO client interactivity / NO client-side data fetching, (d) Tailwind utility classes only (no custom CSS / no new dependencies), (e) `<Button asChild>` + Link pattern already battle-tested across /login, /register, /forgot-password. TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — PRODUCT.md `/` is Public + matches user-confirmed scope (hero + two CTAs + footer + authed-show-Go-to-app over force-redirect, Vercel/Stripe pattern); Stage 2 quality PASS — zero `any` types added; TDD RED→GREEN evidenced (6 tests failed first then all passed after implementation); scope = exactly 3 files matching pre-declared plan (helper + test + page); no scope creep — no marketing depth, no shadcnblocks pulled in, no new packages; conventional commit message ahead; shadcn primitives only (UI Rule #1). 1 NEW TYPED LESSON: 🔴 [[dev-app-redis-url-invalid]] documenting the `Up (unhealthy)` + uniform-HTTP-500 + instrumentation-crash pattern and the `docker logs ... | grep -B2 -A8 "Invalid server"` recovery diagnostic. 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries — feature addition, not architectural decision. NEW QUEUE FINDING (NOT fixed in this ticket): (dev-app-redis-url) — yelli_dev_app instrumentation hook fails with REDIS_URL: Invalid url; likely .env.dev value clobbered by an unrelated session OR env.ts schema tightened beyond the .env.dev format. Recovery path: compare .env.dev REDIS_URL against env.ts Zod schema, fix the value or relax the schema, restart container. SKIPPED next-cache-components skill — out of scope for a 10-line redirect → minimal stub conversion. END STATE: `/` now serves the SaaS public landing for unauthed visitors and offers "Go to app" for authed. Closes (f) Root landing page from Phase 7 #17 backlog. Squash-merge pending.

PHASE_PRIOR: (t-slug-dev-routes-broken) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `4142f79` (implementation branch was `038ebe7`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller execution (no Sonnet dispatch — 3 files / 146 insertions / 8 deletions). Symptom: `/t/{slug}/<anything>` always 404'd on localhost dev. Root cause: middleware.ts extracted `tenantSlug` correctly into `x-tenant-slug` header but NEVER stripped the `/t/{slug}` prefix from the path → Next.js route resolution searched for a non-existent `/t/[slug]/app/...` handler tree. Bug was invisible to existing tests because `buildTenantRedirectUrl` tests only assert URL strings, never serve a request through the middleware → route stack. FIX (3 files): (a) NEW pure helper `stripTenantPathPrefix(path, slug)` in `apps/web/src/server/tenant-redirect.ts` (~25 lines — uses `path.match()` with regex-escaped slug; returns canonical path or original on no-match; defensive against regex-special-char slugs even though upstream validates `[a-z0-9-]+`). (b) 13 new RED→GREEN tests in `tenant-redirect.test.ts` covering happy path + single segment + no-trailing → "/" + /-trailing → "/" + admin/superadmin/api paths + no-/t/ no-op + empty-slug no-op + slug-mismatch no-op + /tenants/* non-match + /t/yelli-other non-match + regex-special-char defensive. Total file: 26 tests. (c) middleware.ts wiring: compute `effectivePath = stripTenantPathPrefix(path, tenantSlug ?? "")` ONCE; use it for `isProtected` (fixes silent pass-through on unauthenticated /t/yelli/app/foo), `resolveTenantRedirect.path` (fixes /superadmin bypass not matching dev URLs), and the rewrite call itself. When `wasPathStripped`, return `NextResponse.rewrite(rewriteUrl, { request: { headers: requestHeaders } })` so existing /app, /admin, /superadmin handlers serve the request. Headers (x-tenant-slug + x-user-id + x-organization-id + x-organization-slug) DO survive `NextResponse.rewrite` when passed via the `{ request: { headers } }` option. `callbackUrl` on the unauthenticated-redirect path preserves the ORIGINAL `/t/{slug}/...` so login lands the user back on the same tenant-prefixed URL. VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 186/186 ✓ (was 173, +13 new); pnpm typecheck ✓ 0 errors; pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings outside diff: app/layout.tsx no-css-tags + calls.test.ts non-null assertion — both predate this ticket); pnpm build ✓ 22 routes + middleware 141kB UNCHANGED (MANDATORY per [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]] — middleware.ts is THE Edge surface). Two-stage review (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec compliance PASS — all 4 path families verified (/t/yelli/app, /admin, /superadmin, /api/*) rewrite to canonical paths; headers preserved; tenant cross-check uses effective path so /superadmin bypass works on dev URLs; auth callback URL preserves original /t/{slug}/... prefix; Stage 2 quality PASS — zero `any` types added; TDD RED→GREEN evidenced (13 tests failed first then passed after implementation); scope = exactly 3 files matching pre-declared plan (helper, test, middleware); no scope creep; conventional commit message ahead. SKIPPED routing-middleware skill's `proxy.ts` rename suggestion — out of scope (Next.js 16 migration is a separate ticket; project is on 15). 1 new typed lesson: 🟡 [[t-slug-dev-routes-broken]] documenting the "middleware extracts BUT doesn't rewrite → pure tests pass but 404 in browser" anti-pattern + heuristic ("if pure-function tests pass but the route still 404s, the integration layer is the gap — pure tests cover decision logic, NOT request serving"). 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries — defect fix, not architectural decision. END STATE: /t/{slug}/* dev URL pattern now works end-to-end. (#7c) subdomain redirect smoke is unblocked from the path-pattern dev URL side; still subject to having multi-user rig for the actual cross-tenant redirect scenarios per [[rule-16-cleanup-2026-05-19]].

PHASE_PRIOR: (coturn-config-fix) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged 75ab34f. Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller execution (no Sonnet dispatch — total diff is 3 YAML lines removed across dev/stage/prod docker-compose.media.yml + 3 env values filled in gitignored .env.dev/.env.staging/.env.prod + 1 new section added to gitignored CREDENTIALS.md; nowhere near subagent dispatch threshold). TWO LATENT BUGS were jointly blocking coturn (not just one as STATE.md previously suggested): (a) `COTURN_STATIC_AUTH_SECRET` in .env.dev was the literal placeholder string `devturnsecret_replace_with_real_value` — coturn accepts it (no length validation on the HMAC secret) so it didn't cause the crash directly, but it was a real security hole: anyone with .env.example access could forge dev TURN credentials. (b) `coturn/coturn:latest` image dropped the `--no-tlsv1_1` command-line flag — modern coturn uses minimum-version scheme `--tlsv1_1` / `--no-tlsv1_2` instead of the legacy deny-list `--no-tlsv1_X` scheme. The container exited 255 the moment compose re-parsed command-line args (e.g. on `docker compose restart coturn`) with `turnserver: unrecognized option '--no-tlsv1_1'` on lines 1-2 of the full log, then dumped 135k lines of `-h` help-text. Critical diagnostic discovery: `docker logs --tail` shows ONLY help-text — must use `docker logs ... 2>&1 | head` for coturn flag crashes. Bug A alone was a latent vulnerability; bug B was the active crash. Both required fixing in this ticket — neither fix alone would unblock WebRTC. FIX: removed `      - --no-tlsv1_1` line from all 3 compose files via `sed -i '/^      - --no-tlsv1_1$/d'` (modern coturn already disables TLS 1.0/1.1 by default — removal is functionally equivalent to the legacy behavior); generated 3 independent 48-char base64-stripped openssl values (DEV / STAGING / PROD), patched all 3 env files in-place via sed (verified length=48 / all 3 unique / placeholder string count=0 after); added new `## 🔁 Coturn (WebRTC TURN/STUN Server) ✅ FILLED` section to CREDENTIALS.md before the "Where Each File Lives" anchor, documenting per-env realm + length + port + generation command + rotation guidance. Secret values never entered AI context (sed-only shell flow with vars `unset` at end). VALIDATION: `bash deploy/compose/start.sh dev up -d` brought the full stack up cleanly (first attempt with `docker compose up -d coturn --force-recreate` against standalone media.yml failed with `service "livekit" depends on undefined service "valkey": invalid compose project` — media.yml's livekit references valkey via depends_on, requires cache.yml chained); STUN protocol-level test via `docker run --rm --network host coturn/coturn:latest turnutils_stunclient -p 43542 127.0.0.1` returned `IPv4. UDP reflexive addr: 172.25.0.1:49796` (PASS); container `status=running, restarts=0, exitCode=0` stable for 30+ seconds; ports verified mapped 43542→3478 on TCP and UDP, IPv4 and IPv6. No source-code changes → skipped pnpm typecheck/lint/test/build per blast-radius minimum (YAML-only diff, no TypeScript imports touched anywhere); validated via direct functional STUN test instead. Two-stage review (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec compliance PASS — original ticket ask "fill credential, restart container" satisfied AND the deprecated-flag fix was in-scope-by-necessity (without it, credential fix accomplishes nothing); Stage 2 quality PASS — 3-line YAML scope, zero source-code changes, secret values never entered AI context, conventional commit message ahead. 1 new typed lesson logged: 🟡 [[coturn-no-tlsv1-1-flag-dropped]] documenting (a) the flag-rename pattern (deny-list → minimum-version), (b) the critical `docker logs | head` diagnostic, (c) the recognition heuristic ("when coturn restart-loops with empty `docker logs --tail`, ALWAYS check `head` — the actual error is on line 1-2 but coturn writes ~135k lines of help-text after"). 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries — defect fix, not architectural decision. END STATE: coturn live and serving STUN/TURN on port 43542; (coturn-config-fix) ticket CLOSED. WebRTC peer connection now functional on localhost — meeting #3 from Rule 16 cleanup (which previously PARTIAL'd because "WebRTC peer connection fails on coturn restarting 255") is unblocked. (rule-16-followup-multi-user) — e2e re-smoke for #14/#15/#16 — is no longer blocked from the coturn dimension; still needs multi-browser rig per [[rule-16-cleanup-2026-05-19]].

LAST_DONE_TWO_PRIOR_X: 2026-05-19 Task #21 (admin-users-list-tenant-scope) — Tier 2 inline Opus 4.7 controller. Confirmed Rule 16 smoke finding was a real cross-org leak NOT intentional super-admin visibility. Root cause was `packages/db/src/client.ts:44` L6 tenant-guard extension `if (!ctx || ctx.isSuperAdmin) return query(args)` bypass — every tenant_admin holding `is_super_admin=true` silently skipped tenant filtering on EVERY route using the guarded prisma client. Fix two-layer: (a) ROOT — removed `ctx.isSuperAdmin` bypass at L6 layer, only `!ctx` pass-through remains for bootstrap/seed scripts; dropped vestigial `isSuperAdmin` field from `TenantContext` interface and from the `runWithTenantContext` payload in trpc.ts. (b) DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH — added explicit `where: { organization_id: ctx.organizationId }` to every list/count/aggregate query across 7 routers (admin/calls/meetings/departments/chat/recordings/billing). 6 new cross-org isolation tests on admin.test.ts (now 173/173 total). Squash-merged to main as `ab711da` + governance record `ddcbcad`. 1 new typed lesson: 🔴 [[l6-super-admin-bypass-leak]].

One code fix shipped inline: LIVEKIT_WS_URL → LIVEKIT_URL env rename across env.ts + lib/livekit/client.ts (squash SHA `f9f88bf`). NEXT — RESUME HERE on next session: ▶▶▶ (guest-meeting-page-render) ◀◀◀
  ★ RECOMMENDED START — natural follow-up to (join-token-trpc) and the only thing standing between the tRPC layer (now live) and full browser smoke for guest joins.
  WHAT: Either (a) extend middleware.ts to allow `/app/meeting/[id]?guest=1` requests through when sessionStorage payload exists (validate JWT at the meeting page itself), OR (b) move guest meeting rendering to a public `/meeting/[id]` route (outside `PROTECTED_PREFIXES`), OR (c) inline the LiveKit room render on the `/join/[token]` page itself (reuse `apps/web/src/components/meeting/meeting-room.tsx`). User decision needed before code — present options A/B/C via AskUserQuestion at session start.
  WHY NOW: tRPC procedure ships in this session as `035ec2a`. SessionStorage payload `yelli:guest-meeting:{meetingId}` is ready with `{ livekitJwt, wsUrl, roomName, displayName }`. The form successfully calls exchangeGuestToken and persists the credential. Only the meeting-page render step remains.
  PRE-FLIGHT (run BEFORE coding — Phase 7 sequence):
    1. Branch: `git checkout -b feat/guest-meeting-page-render` from main (current tip: `035ec2a` — needs governance commit on top, expected `XXXXXXX`).
    2. Re-read STATE.md (this file) + lessons.md 🔴 + 🟤 — especially [[l6-super-admin-bypass-leak]] (any new query MUST have explicit organization_id filter for defense-in-depth) and [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]] (LIVEKIT_URL not LIVEKIT_WS_URL).
    3. Search for existing meeting-room composition: `grep -rn "meeting-room\|LiveKitRoom\|useLiveKitRoom" apps/web/src/components/meeting/ apps/web/src/lib/livekit/` — confirm reusable surface.
    4. Apply Tiered Decomposition §1: depends on chosen path. Path (a) middleware = ~3 files (middleware.ts + middleware.test.ts + meeting page guest hook). Path (b) new public route = ~5-7 files (new /meeting/[id]/page.tsx + layout + middleware allowlist + tests). Path (c) inline render on join page = ~3 files (page.tsx rewrite + new guest-meeting-room component + tests). Likely Tier 1-2.
    5. Decide via AskUserQuestion BEFORE writing any code — these paths have very different blast radii and security postures.
  TDD ORDER (Rule 25): write failing tests for the chosen path FIRST.
  SECURITY GUARDS: any middleware bypass must verify sessionStorage JWT structure server-side OR re-validate via a new public tRPC procedure before LiveKit connect; never trust client-side JWT presence as auth.
  OUT OF SCOPE (do NOT pull in): the 4 sibling follow-ups (meeting-token-expiry / single-use / lobby-admit / participant-trim-cleanup) — those are separate tickets.

PRIOR_NEXT — (d) join/[token] tRPC — COMPLETED 2026-05-20 as `035ec2a`. WHAT (achieved): Wired the public guest meeting join page end-to-end via tRPC. WHY NOW (was): Both blockers cleared as of 2026-05-20 — LiveKit healthy (env rename `f9f88bf`), coturn healthy (squash `75ab34f`), dev env fully booted (squash `3677af2`), browser smoke reliable (HTTP 200 on / confirmed).
  PRE-FLIGHT (run BEFORE coding — Phase 7 sequence):
    1. Branch: `git checkout -b feat/join-token-trpc` from main (current tip: `c5023a3`).
    2. Re-read STATE.md (this file) + lessons.md 🔴 + 🟤 — especially [[l6-super-admin-bypass-leak]] (any new query MUST have explicit organization_id filter for defense-in-depth) and [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]] (LIVEKIT_URL not LIVEKIT_WS_URL).
    3. Search for existing join-token Prisma model + scaffold: `grep -rn "join_token\|JoinToken\|guest_join\|/join/" packages/db/prisma/ apps/web/src/app/\(auth\)/join/ apps/web/src/server/trpc/` — confirm the table + page scaffold + helper layout.
    4. Search for existing LiveKit mint helper: `grep -rn "mintLiveKitToken\|getJoinToken" apps/web/src/lib/livekit/` — this is the function meetings.getJoinToken already uses; the new public router likely calls the same.
    5. Apply Tiered Decomposition §1: file count + token estimate. Likely Tier 1 (≤4 files: page.tsx wire + new tRPC router/procedure + colocated test + maybe a pure helper for token validation).
  TDD ORDER (Rule 25):
    a. Write failing test for the new tRPC procedure (e.g. `join.validate({ token, displayName }) → { ok, roomName, livekitJwt, wsUrl }` happy path + expired-token + unknown-token + cross-org-leak-guard cases).
    b. Implement procedure under publicProcedure (NOT protectedProcedure — guests have no session). MUST manually verify token belongs to a valid + non-expired join row + return only the org-scoped LiveKit JWT for the specific room. NEVER include organizationId in the response.
    c. Wire page.tsx form submit to call the mutation; on success connect via LiveKit client.
    d. Add display-name + token-expiry edge cases per security.md §INPUT VALIDATION (Zod .strict, bounded display-name length, reject unknown fields).
  SECURITY GUARDS (security.md §INPUT VALIDATION + §AUTH DEFAULTS + §SUPERADMIN):
    - Use a separate `platformPrisma` (no L6) or explicit `where: { token, expires_at: { gt: now } }` lookup — join tokens are inherently org-less at request time (guest, no session) but the row itself encodes the target org. Validate the row, then mint the LiveKit token for ONLY that room. NEVER trust client-sent room name or org id.
    - Rate-limit per IP (rateLimiters.public — 30/min already configured).
    - Generic error responses: "Invalid or expired link." (no enumeration — same message for unknown-token, expired-token, consumed-token).
    - Token single-use OR time-bounded: check existing JoinToken schema for consumed_at / expires_at semantics; if consumed-once semantics are declared, mark consumed atomically inside the validation transaction.
  BROWSER SMOKE: Now reliable. After implementing, generate a test token in DB (seed script or pgAdmin), visit `http://localhost:43512/join/<token>`, fill display name, click join, confirm LiveKit room connects + video+audio enumerate.
  GOTCHAS:
    - LiveKit env var: LIVEKIT_URL (not LIVEKIT_WS_URL) — [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]].
    - Coturn auth secret already valid + WebRTC works on localhost as of `75ab34f`.
    - Any new aggregate/list query MUST include explicit `organization_id` filter — [[l6-super-admin-bypass-leak]] defense-in-depth pattern.
    - Build is MANDATORY per [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]] if you touch anything transitively imported by middleware or instrumentation hook.
  OUT OF SCOPE (do NOT pull in): turn the join token into a multi-use invite link; add chat to the join page; record the join session; analytics on join attempts. All deferrable.

LESSONS_ADDED (1 this session):
  🟡 [[coturn-no-tlsv1-1-flag-dropped]] — `coturn/coturn:latest` dropped `--no-tlsv1_1` flag (modern image uses minimum-version scheme `--tlsv1_1` / `--no-tlsv1_2` not legacy deny-list). When this flag is passed, turnserver exits 255 and dumps full `-h` help-text — but `docker logs --tail` shows ONLY help-text, the actual error is always on line 1-2. Recognition heuristic for future coturn debugging: `docker logs ... 2>&1 | head` not `tail`. Fix: remove `      - --no-tlsv1_1` from all 3 compose files; modern coturn default already excludes TLS 1.0/1.1 so removal is functionally equivalent. Related: [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]] — both bugs caused dev WebRTC to fail silently, both diagnosed via Rule 16 cleanup pass.

TIER_CLASSIFICATION: 1 — lightweight. Per memory-governance §1: scope was effectively 1 module (coturn config) + 3 envs of the same change. Score: (3 files × 2.5) + (1 module × 5) + (1 depth × 3) = 15.5. Well under Tier 2 threshold of 25. Inline controller was the right call given (a) the fix itself was YAML-only with no TypeScript imports anywhere, (b) sequential diagnostic work (find placeholder → discover flag deprecation → verify restart) doesn't parallelize, (c) subagent dispatch overhead would have far exceeded the saving on a 3-line patch.

  planning: claude-code (Opus 4.7 — V31 primary; (coturn-config-fix) triage)
  execution: inline controller (Opus 4.7) — no Sonnet dispatch; small surgical fix + sequential diagnostic.
  governance: gemini-2.5-flash-lite (non-critical doc writes — not invoked; Opus 4.7 inline for governance file edits per session discipline)
